name: wallet-selection-hl-consistency
description: Hyperliquid wallet-selection/rating config. 'Best' = the steady risk-adjusted earner, NOT the peak-return hero. Prioritizes Sharpe/Sortino/Calmar, cross-window consistency (positive in MOST of the six 60-day windows, not one lucky burst), low max drawdown, and a controlled left tail. This operationalizes the single defensible finding of HL_PERSISTENCE_STUDY_2026-06: risk-adjusted (consistency) ranking is 'the only method with a defensible forward profile' — same pooled IC as raw-PnL ranking (+0.116 vs +0.124) but it REMOVES the catastrophic left tail (worst forward window goes from -39% mean to -6.6%). It directly attacks the 'wallet-2 two-bursts problem': a wallet whose annual PnL is really two short bursts (best-window concentration > 1.0) is penalized, not rewarded. Decision-support ONLY — surfaces a small shortlist of defensible books for MANUAL review. HONEST PRIOR: HL copy-trading was rejected five ways; this tool does not resurrect it, it just ranks the survivors least-badly.
changelog: 0.1.0 — initial HL consistency ranker. Metrics computed from data/backups/hyperliquid-study/windows.json (per-window return, avgAccountValue, dailyVol over six 60d windows) + persistent-set.json (Sharpe/maxDD/concentration cross-check). Adapts the Solana tail-discipline schema (riskDiscipline/performance/ratingRubric/autoDisqualifiers/0-10) to HL window-series metrics. Tuned so the persistent-set 'clean book' 0x5559da (Sharpe 2.5, maxDD 28%) grades high and lottery members (Sharpe < 0.5, best5StepsShare > 1.0, one blew up -367%) grade C-F.

dataSource._doc: All metrics are computable OFFLINE from the cached study artifacts. No live calls.
dataSource.windowSeries: six consecutive 60-day windows anchored 2026-06-12 (w0 2025-06-17 .. w5 2026-06-12); per-window return = d(cumPnL)/timeWeightedAvgAccountValue; dailyVol = QV-estimator within-window daily PnL vol normalized by avg account value

eligibility._doc: Hard cutoffs to even be scored. The whole philosophy rests on having ENOUGH windows to judge consistency — a 2-window account cannot be shown to be 'steady'. We demand the FULL six, not the study's minimum-four, because consistency is the thesis.
eligibility._note: minUsableWindows: 6 (study allowed 4). minAvgAccountValueUsd raised from study's $10k to $25k to exclude micro-books whose 'returns' are noise.

riskDiscipline._doc: PRIMARY criteria for THIS philosophy. The danger we bound is NOT a martingale's add-depth (the Solana config's concern) — on HL with only window-resolution data we cannot see adds. The danger here is HIDDEN LEFT-TAIL VARIANCE: a book that looks great on total return because two windows printed and the rest bled. We bound it via drawdown, worst-window return, and within-window vol.

maxDrawdownFrac._doc: Peak-to-trough on the cumulative-window-return path, as a fraction of avg account value. Study median for the persistent set was 1.32 (132%!) — these are violent books. We grade HARD here: A = a genuinely smooth book.

worstWindowReturn._doc: The single worst 60-day window return. The whole point of consistency is that the bad windows are SHALLOW. A wallet with a -300% window is one stop away from zero. (Negative = loss; thresholds are floors, e.g. gradeA requires worst window >= -0.15.)

avgWithinWindowVol._doc: Mean of the six per-window dailyVol values (QV daily PnL vol / avg account value). Smoothness of the equity curve INSIDE windows, not just across them. Lower = calmer book.

everBlewUp._doc: Any window return <= -1.0 (lost >100% of avg account value in 60d) OR a final cumulative path that went non-positive after being positive. The persistent set had a member (0xe4d1fa) blow up -367% AFTER qualifying — that is the exact tail this philosophy refuses to follow.

performance._doc: SECONDARY. Risk-ADJUSTED return is the headline; raw return is bounded — extreme total return is treated with suspicion because on HL it signals lottery variance (study: P(top->bottom) = 27.7%, a barbell), not a steady earner. We want a good Sharpe with a MODERATE return, not a moonshot.

sharpeAnnual._doc: Annualized Sharpe from the six window returns (mean/std, scaled to annual by sqrt(windows-per-year=6.08)). THE headline metric. Study persistent-set median 1.37; only 9 of 43 >= 2.0.

sortinoAnnual._doc: Like Sharpe but denominator is downside deviation (only negative window returns). Rewards books whose volatility is mostly upside. Often higher than Sharpe for a true edge.

calmar._doc: Total study-period return / maxDrawdownFrac. Reward per unit of worst pain. The cleanest single risk-adjusted summary for a copier who fears the drawdown.

totalReturnSanity._doc: Return is BOUNDED on both ends. A floor (must actually make money) and a CEILING above which we get suspicious it is lottery variance, not skill, and cap the profitability grade. Mirrors the Solana config's bounded win-rate idea.

consistency._doc: THE defining category of this philosophy — weighted heaviest. Directly attacks the wallet-2 two-bursts problem.

positiveWindowFraction._doc: Fraction of the six windows with a positive return. 'Positive in MOST windows' is the literal thesis. A = 5-6 of 6; the two-bursts wallet (2 of 6 positive) lands at D.

bestWindowConcentration._doc: Share of total POSITIVE profit coming from the single best window. Low = profit spread across windows (steady). High (>1.0 possible when other windows are net-negative) = the burst signature. Study best5StepsShare median ~1.22 — most persistent books fail this. (Thresholds are ceilings: gradeA requires concentration <= 0.40.)

returnSignStability._doc: 1 - (number of sign flips in the window-return sequence / max possible flips). A book that alternates win/lose/win/lose is unpredictable to follow even if net positive. Higher = more stable trajectory.

ratingRubric._doc: Per-category letter grades A-F (A=4..F=0). Overall = weighted average; ANY autoDisqualifier floors overall to F. Consistency + risk-adjusted performance dominate the weights, by design.

scale._doc: Tiny weight. A bigger, longer-lived book is marginally more trustworthy as a real operator (skin in the game) and more copyable; but this is NOT a return amplifier and is deliberately near-zero weight so it cannot rescue a streaky book.

autoDisqualifiers._doc: Any TRUE => overall grade F + 'DISQUALIFIED'. These encode the persistence-study lessons: blow-ups, deep single-window losses, and the two-bursts profile are non-starters for a 'steady earner' thesis regardless of headline return.

overall._doc: Emit BOTH a letter and a 0-10 score per category and overall. A disqualified wallet shows F/0 with the breached rule(s) listed. 0-10 = (weighted GPA / 4) * 10.

uiHints._doc: Columns/badges for a trader-browser UI so a human can scan and manually shortlist.

badgeThresholds._doc: Machine-readable badge cutoffs the scorer evaluates (JSON is the source of truth; mirrors _badgeRules above).
